mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate increases above 3.5% signal monetary tightening that raises discount rates on equity valuations and increases borrowing costs for corporations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 158,736 thousand as of May 2022
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of months following PAYEMS declines, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in housing starts below 1,400 thousand units signals weakening residential demand that reduces construction employment, material demand, and consumer wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,502 thousand as of April 2022
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when HOUST < 1400, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declining month-over-month combined with rising inflation signals stagflation conditions where supply constraints persist despite weakening demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.50 and CPIAUCSL at 332.41 as of May 2022
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of stagflation-triggered instances, or the joint effect of INDPRO decline and high CPIAUCSL shows no significant interaction term (p > 0.15) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while unemployment remains elevated above 4.0%, signaling consumer demand weakness that reduces corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 757,085 million and UNRATE at 4.3% as of May 2022
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of dual-trigger instances, or the interaction coefficient between RSAFS growth and UNRATE is not statistically significant (p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate in the most recent quarter, signaling economic slowdown that reduces equity earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 24,174.53 billion as of Q1 2022
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of periods with sub-1.5% GDP growth, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by year-over-year CPI growth above 8.0% combined with federal funds rate below 4.0% signals monetary policy lag where real rates remain negative, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as inflation erodes debt burdens and boosts nominal earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 332.41 and FEDFUNDS at 3.64% as of May 2022
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of negative real rate instances, or the interaction term between high inflation and low nominal rates is not statistically significant (p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period while payroll employment declines, signaling labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.3pp over 3 months AND PAYEMS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.3% and PAYEMS at 158,736 thousand as of May 2022
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of dual labor-weakness instances, or the joint effect of rising unemployment and falling payrolls shows no significant interaction (p > 0.15) in predictive models.
